title: Could a community-driven repository be created where everyone contributes their knowledge to build a bot?
author: SnooDoggos3286
selftext:
I understand that some people want to sell their bots, and that’s totally fine. But maybe there are others who are interested in creating an open-source one and refining it collaboratively.

Or is it just too frustrating to get everyone aligned?

I’m asking in case something like this has been tried before, or if the moment someone makes it profitable, they simply stop sharing.

What are your thoughts? Thanks in advance for your input.

comments.author: littleDevX
comments.body:
Hey mate, how are you?
I am building and improving the bots i am building based on success and feedback “in public” in my discord server. How i calculate scores and which ta i am using for filtering etc. 
I really like your idea, but most likely its difficult to find people who are willing to share a successful setup for free.
Hopefully ill get there soon and the community can grow. Feel free to join and play around with the bots (they are not live for autotrading or fishy things) 
Cheers
